title: Russell Street Bombing: The 1986 terror attack that shook Melbourne | 9 News Australia
description: On March 27, 1986, a car bomb detonated outside the Russell Street Police Headquarters in the heart of Melbourne. The explosion injured 22 people and ultimately claimed the life of 21-year-old Constable Angela Taylor, the first Australian policewoman killed in the line of duty.This archival timeline follows the immediate chaos of the blast, the massive forensic investigation involving 100 personnel, and the eventual trial that brought those responsible to justice. From the initial reports by Brian Naylor to the emotional farewell for Constable Taylor, relive the events that changed the city forever. #9News   00:00 40 years on: Remembering the Russell Street bombing 02:24 Initial reports: Brian Naylor on the "City Terror" 02:56 Chaos on Russell Street: First images of the blast 04:37 Senior Constable Janeck and witnesses describe the shock 06:05 Victoria Police launch one of the city's biggest operations 06:59 Paul Murphy reports from Melbourne’s hospitals 08:34 "Color Blue": The tape-recorded warning sent to 9News 09:41 Comparing the attack to the 1978 Hilton bombing 10:22 Prime Minister Bob Hawke and Premier John Cain condemn the violence 11:38 Internal damage: A look inside the police complex 15:24 Forensic teams find a chunk of the bomb car a block away 17:09 Reconstructing the stolen Holden Commodore 18:35 A multi-coloured travelling rug: A vital clue to the bomber 20:35 Constable Angela Taylor dies in hospital 21:14 A state funeral for Constable Taylor 23:37 Raids in Swan Hill: Arrests made by the Special Operations Group 25:16 Trial and verdict: Justice for Angela Taylor 26:36 Legacy: The Angela Rose Taylor Rose Garden   Subscribe and �: http://9Soci.al/KM6e50GjSK9 | Get more breaking news at 9News.com.au: http://9Soci.al/iyCO50GjSK6  FOLLOW 9News Australia ► Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/9News/ ► Twitter: https://twitter.com/9NewsAUS ► Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/9news/  Join 9News for the latest in news and events that affect you in your local city, as well as news from across Australia and the world.  #BREAKINGNEWS #9NEWS #AUSTRALIA
